label: Corporate Grid
description: B2B, enterprise and consulting landing pages for agencies, law firms, finance and professional services. Emphasizes trust signals, partner logos, case studies, and formal credibility — built for compliance, enterprise SaaS, accounting, legal and management consulting where clients need reassurance before action.
signatureMotif: B2B trust signals, partner logos, enterprise case studies, and measured consulting hierarchy on a precise neutral grid
promptHints: Use this variant for B2B, enterprise, consulting, agency, finance, legal, accounting and professional-services prompts where trust and formal credibility matter more than expressive atmospherics.
Lead with business clarity, partner trust, and a measured hierarchy — not lifestyle mood, startup velocity, or local warmth.
Prefer proof blocks (KPI metrics, client logos, case studies, compliance badges) over decorative illustration cards.
Keep font weights restrained — bold for headings, regular for body. Avoid playful type mixing.

layouts: Use a 60/40 hero split with left-aligned headline and proof bullets, paired with a right-side dashboard screenshot or client-results card.
Place a single-row logo strip directly under the hero, followed by a three-column trust band for metrics, certifications, or service guarantees.
Build services as a strict three-card grid with equal heights, restrained icons, short summaries, and one consistent CTA row alignment.
Use alternating case-study sections with a text block on one side and a bordered outcome panel on the other.
Structure pricing or engagement models as a three-column table, with the middle option slightly raised but still visually conservative.
motifs: Use bright white cards on pale neutral backgrounds with 1px cool-gray borders, soft shadow, and medium rounded corners.
Keep all sections locked to a visible grid rhythm with consistent column widths, even gutters, and generous horizontal padding.
Favor proof-first components like logo bars, KPI stats, testimonial quotes, and compliance badges over decorative illustrations.
Use restrained blue accents for links, buttons, and highlights while keeping the overall page predominantly neutral and light.
antiPatterns: Avoid playful collage layouts, tilted cards, or overlapping sticker elements that weaken enterprise trust and formal clarity.
Never lead with abstract 3D blobs, loud gradients, or expressive hero art before showing business proof or service value.
Avoid oversized display typography, mixed font personalities, or editorial text treatments that feel more lifestyle than corporate.
Do not use dense dark-mode hero blocks or black-heavy sections that break the variant’s light, measured consulting tone.
